title: Margaritopsis anacamptopus
taxonomy: Margaritopsis anacamptopus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family includes a vast array of plants, from coffee and gardenias to the Madagascar periwinkle. The genus Margaritopsis itself is part of the tribe Psychotrieae, which contains around 1,400 species of flowering plants. Further classification of Margaritopsis anacamptopus may include its specific rank within the family and genus, but its placement within the tribe and family is well established.

intro: Margaritopsis anacamptopus, commonly known as 'Kava Cordyline' or 'Pacific Kava', is a plant species native to the Pacific Islands, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation, and alleviating pain.
